title: lobelia koolauensis
intro: Lobelia koolauensis, also known as Ma'oh, is a plant species endemic to Hawaii and a member of the Campanulaceae family, commonly referred to as the bellflower family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits associated with Lobelia koolauensis include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to alleviate pain and reduce fever. Lobelia koolauensis can be prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential toxicity when ingested in large quantities.
